title: Tillandsia goudae
intro: Tillandsia goudae, also known as Gouda's Airplant, is a species of epiphytic bromeliad, characterized by its unique ability to thrive without soil by absorbing moisture and nutrients through its leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Tillandsia goudae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Tillandsia goudae include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to treat various ailments, including coughs, sore throats, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Tillandsia goudae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae, commonly known as the pineapple family. This epiphyte is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. Tillandsia goudae is a part of the Tillandsia genus, which consists of around 650 species of air plants. The plant's classification places it within the Bromelioideae subfamily, characterized by its unique adaptations to absorb moisture and nutrients from the air. As a member of the Bromeliaceae family, Tillandsia goudae exhibits a range of morphological and anatomical features that are typical of the group.
